programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. programme.desc: Cathy is forced by HR to take compassionate leave and see a bereavement councillor, but she is too scared to tell Tess, so Cat says she should pretend she's going to work but come and hang out at her house instead. Cathy agrees and returns home to find Tess taking over yet more of the wedding planning, making her anxious again. Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Period mystery set in 19th-century New Zealand at the height of the 1860s gold rush, and based on the Man Booker Prize-winning novel by Eleanor Catton. Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the day's three Premier League fixtures, which were Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Aston Villa v Chelsea at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Sheffield United at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds were looking to record their third consecutive victory in all competitions against their bitter rivals, and the omens looked good with the Toffees' last triumph coming way back in October 2010. Villa had a similarly poor recent record against Chelsea, having lost their previous five meetings, while the Blades hadn't beaten the Magpies since November 2006.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Father's Day
programme.desc: Ahead of a Father's Day with a difference his year, the comedian sets up for a day on the road outside Michael's front door. With social distancing taken into account, the two of them will spend some quality time together as best they can, look at some old family photos, awkward home video footage and tell some classic Whitehall tales.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Gloucester Antiques Centre, where the red and blue teams go shopping for treasures with experts Kate Bliss and Charles Hanson. Away from the contest, Charlie learns about astronomer William Herschel, who discovered the planet Uranus.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boards one of the world's most famous trains, the Rocky Mountaineer, to cross the backbone of the North American continent from Kamloops to the spa resort of Banff. This magnificent journey takes him to the highest point of the 19th century transcontinental Canadian Pacific Railway line at Kicking Horse Pass, past Lake Louise and inside spiral tunnels blasted through the mountains. Along the way, Michael hears of the harsh and dangerous conditions endured by the Chinese and European labourers who built the railway, and looks back at the historic driving of the Last Spike, which completed the Canadian Pacific Railway in 1885.(n)

programme.title: The Home That Two Built
programme.desc: The documentary tracking 50 years of lifestyle and homemaking programmes on BBC Two concludes by looking at the 1990s and 2000s, when homes were transformed by chucking out the chintz and gardens were turned into outside rooms. Soon there was a craze for decluttering and minimal living, along with a slew of programmes about buying and selling property, homes in the country and living abroad. Featuring contributions by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Diarmuid Gavin and Charlie Dimmock. Mel Giedroyc narrates.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen head to Monmouthshire, to sample the mouthwatering delights of Abergavenny. The pair are impressed by the variety of locally produced food on offer in the market town known as the gateway to Wales and feast on a Nepalese curry, drink Welsh wine, try making sweets that the miners would have taken down the pit and drop into a Michelin-starred restaurant. They also attend a barbecue at the castle, where they are serenaded by a male voice choir.(n)

programme.title: World Beaters
programme.desc: England defender Lucy tells how she fought back from injury to represent her country.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Germany v Portugal. A chance to see the Euro 2008 quarter-final at St Jakob-Park in Basel, Switzerland.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from Powderham Castle in Devon. Dating back to the 14th century, it is the long standing home of the Courtenay family.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In picturesque Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon. En route, he also visits the home of British mountaineering, Pen y Gwryd, to hear how an Edwardian journalist and poet created a climbing community, which grew to include men who would conquer Everest in the 1950s.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines the remotest part of the solar system, a place that the most mysterious planets - Uranus, Neptune and dwarf planet Pluto - call home. Barely visible to the naked eye, Uranus was once thought to be the furthest planet from the Sun, before Neptune was discovered. Thanks to a rare alignment of the planets in 1976, Voyager 2 was sent for mankind's only flyby of these ice worlds. Even at such cold temperatures, great storms whip around these frozen worlds, which are home to spectacular moons and intricate ring systems. Then in 2015, Nasa's New Horizons probe pushed the frontier even further into space with its extraordinary passage to the dwarf planet Pluto.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on growing tomatoes and has ideas of how to fill gaps in the garden once the flowers of spring are over. Joe Swift visits the small city garden of an interior designer where some unusual plants have been used to add an element of surprise to the outdoor space, while in Kent Arit Anderson meets a community whose members have all been prescribed gardening as part of their recovery from physical and mental illness.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cameraman Charlie Hamilton James has been captivated by otters for the last 25 years and has filmed them more than anyone else. Now, through the eyes of three river orphaned otters, he sets out to reveal their survival secrets and exactly why he believes these creatures are so special.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools chats to singer, songwriter, broadcaster and frontman of Elbow, Guy Garvey, who has a long history with Late, having performed six times - the first back in 2003, and most recently last year when Elbow released their eighth studio album Giants of All Sizes. Alongside making music, Guy presents a weekly show on BBC 6 Music, so he is no stranger to delving into the back catalogues to bring listeners his favourite music, and he does just that here, illustrated with treats from the Later archive.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Selma (2014)
programme.desc: Although the Civil Rights Act of 1964 legally desegregates the South, discrimination remains rampant in certain areas, making it very difficult for blacks to register to vote. In 1965, amidst a tense atmosphere of unrest, civil rights campaigner Dr Martin Luther King Jr plans a peaceful march across Alabama from Selma to Montgomery. He is pressured to cancel it, but persists as his goal is to persuade President Lyndon B Johnson to change the law, ensuring black people can register and enjoy full democratic rights. Fact-based drama, starring David Oyelowo, Tom Wilkinson, Tim Roth and Carmen Ejogo.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The team examines the mental health benefits of nature, and follows the fortunes of Choughs in West Wales. Gordon Buchanan focuses on Scottish Ospreys in Loch Lomond National Park, while Chris Packham shares an extraordinary story of how a love of birds united English POWs and German Officers during the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampires attend their first human Super Bowl party, while Guillermo's search for virgins uncovers a deadly threat. Comedy, starring Kayvan Novak, Matt Berry and Harvey Guillen.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampire room-mates fear they have been electronically cursed, leaving Guillermo to thwart a deadly threat to his masters. Comedy, starring Kayvan Novak and Harvey Guillen.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Izzy plans a candy-coloured deliciously sweet extravaganza as a treat for her mum after a terrible year which saw her parent diagnosed with cancer.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce a collection of weird clips from the natural world, featuring a solar death ray that's frying birds mid-flight, how the sun changed the course of musical history with a violin worth 15 million dollars, and a shocking new discovery - spider goats.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie goes on an offbeat road trip across the UK, which includes a tour of Orkney.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Blue Peter: Six Badges of Summer - Sport Week!
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall sets out to visit six continents in search of Earth's 60 deadliest animals.(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist examines the building blocks of life that have been taken from the Earth's elements, including the pure sulphur mined in the heart of a volcano in Indonesia. On a precipitous dam wall in Italy, mountain goats seek out the chemicals essential to their survival, while in a Japanese bay, Brian explains how the dazzling display of thousands of glowing squid shows how life has utilised the planet's chemistry for its own ends.(n)

programme.title: Bears (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary that follows an Alaskan bear family as the young cubs are taught life's most important lessons. Narrated by John C Reilly.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: The women go back to 1983 to learn how an unsung army of female workers fought to keep their jobs in the face of foreign competition. A change in government at the end of the 70s had brought a dramatic change in economic policy. The Conservatives' policy of encouraging businesses to stand on their own two feet without financial support from government meant that many of the factories in the south Wales valleys began to struggle. Throughout the early 80s, big companies closed their factories and those that managed to stay open were battling against the steady march of globalisation and cheap labour abroad. Presented by Alex Jones.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain samples what London has to offer, heading to Beckton in the east of the city to visit an indoor urban farm in an industrial estate that uses hydroponics and aquaponics to rear fish and grow vegetables. She then heads north to Holloway, to meet chef Pratap Chatal, who runs supper clubs in his home where he showcases his revolutionary idea of cooking his food with fragrances.(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers forage for ingredients as they explore the history of Britain's relationship with wild food. The bikers then prepare a selection of mouth-watering dishes using the best free produce the nation has to offer.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites actor and TV presenter Christopher Biggins to reminisce about his youth in Salisbury. He describes a flamboyant childhood, where he developed an early taste for the high life and was taught the appreciation of food by larger-than-life aunts and grannies. The chef then cooks two nostalgic, mouth-watering dishes, hoping to transport Biggins back to happy times.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley searching for three skip-bound items. Bruce Faulseit repurposes copper pipes, while Daniel Heath comes up with a radical redesign for two old tables.(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Thoms have travelled the world with the RAF, but now Lori dreams of settling in Australia, while husband Kev wants to stay in Scotland. A week in Melbourne may help them decide.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the four remaining contestants take part in the semi final, creating garments inspired by silver screen. First, the semi-finalists must follow a pattern like none they have encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a dress inspired by Marilyn Monroe's in The Seven Year Itch. In the Transformation Challenge, the sewers have to make a sci-fi inspired outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a made to measure flapper dress for the final task of the semi final.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a three-bed home in Surrey, which has a poorly positioned front door and a cluttered hallway. The owners are divided over whether an extension would solve their problems, and the architects present them with two very different visions for what their home could be. Laura takes a characteristically pragmatic approach with a simple but striking contemporary design. But experimental architect Robert decides a far more radical approach is the only answer.(n)

programme.title: Cricket: England v West Indies Greatest Matches
programme.desc: A look back at the Fourth Test of the 1995 series at Old Trafford, where Windies batsman Brian Lara and England fast bowler Dominic Cork took centre-stage.(n)

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: John and Colette cope with the Scottish Cup final crowds trying to smuggle alcohol on dry trains and the station staff deal with angry customers as the summer heat causes havoc on the tracks. A sniffer dog discovers a suspicious package, tour guide Paul uncovers a Victorian platform deep in the bowels of the building and Craig takes a musical journey on the TransPennine Express.(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun in Colour
programme.desc: Egyptologist Elizabeth Frood takes viewers back to 1922 to experience the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb as it happened, with the help of colourised photographs and film footage. The documentary follows the story from the first encounter between Howard Carter and Lord Carnarvon, the discovery of a vast chamber filled with ancient relics to the opening of the burial chamber itself, and reveals how the discoveries made changed how the boy king and Egypt as a nation were viewed around the world.(n)

programme.title: I Am Not Your Negro (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary-maker Raoul Peck uses an unfinished manuscript by James Baldwin, an account of the lives and successive assassinations of civil rights leaders Medgar Evans, Malcolm X and Martin Luther King Jr, as the springboard for this examination of the black experience in America. Narrated by Samuel L Jackson, the film uses Baldwin's words as it explores everything from Hollywood stereotypes to police brutality.(n)

programme.title: Black Hollywood: 'They've Gotta Have Us'
programme.desc: Photographer and filmmaker Simon Frederick guides viewers through conversations with contributors as film veterans Harry Belafonte, Earl Cameron and Diahann Carroll discuss what it was like to be pioneers in a white movie world.(n)

programme.title: Black Hollywood: 'They've Gotta Have Us'
programme.sub-title: Black Film Is Not a Genre
programme.desc: The second episode focuses on how a new generation of black film-makers emerged in the 1990s, following the success of Spike Lee's Do the Right Thing, as well as the development of a new genre, the Hood movie, exploring contemporary black working-class life. The programme also explores how movie stars like Eddie Murphy, Will Smith and Denzel Washington became marquee names, even as black stories were increasingly told by white film-makers. Featuring contributions from David Oyelowo, Laurence Fishburne and John Boyega.(n)

programme.title: Black Hollywood: 'They've Gotta Have Us'
programme.desc: A look ahead to the future facing black film-makers, examining how the impact of high-profile success stories like Black Panther and the Oscar-winning Moonlight and Get Out may bring an end to the belief that black directors and actors are not seen as bankable. The programme also examines a new generation of British talent, including director Steve McQueen and actors David Oyelowo and John Boyega.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical tour of the BBC's culinary archive reaches the letter O. Jamie Oliver prepares baked onions and Michel Roux rustles up a pear omelette souffle with salted butter and caramel sauce.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As well as tending to his crops, much of Adam Henson's time on his Cotswold farm is devoted to caring for his animals. Each and every one of them is important to him, from his commercial flock of sheep to his chickens, and from his pigs to his ponies, with many of them having become much-loved characters to the show's viewers. This special edition of Countryfile takes a journey into the archives to revisit and rediscover some of Adam's highs and lows with his marvellous menagerie of creatures.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on growing tomatoes and has ideas of how to fill gaps in the garden once the flowers of spring are over. Joe Swift visits the small city garden of an interior designer where some unusual plants have been used to add an element of surprise to the outdoor space, while in Kent Arit Anderson meets a community whose members have all been prescribed gardening as part of their recovery from physical and mental illness.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: The team celebrates the Royal Highland Show, which has been cancelled this year. Dougie Vipond charts its 180-year history and Arlene Stuart hears from show regulars at home.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George demonstrates how to detect and deal with garden pests and Kirsty Wilson creates an indoor succulent tower.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. Here, Fred heads to Marrakesh in the company of TV chef Andi Oliver, whose love of soul food has led to an obsession with the city's rich culinary heritage. The maitre d' reveals he has been to Marrakesh before but has yet to have a truly memorable meal. Can Andi change his mind as they are served lamb and couscous at a legendary family restaurant, chicken tagine for under £10, and breakfast on a spectacular roof terrace?(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from TV chefs, including the likes of Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson and Lorraine Pascale.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Rory Bremner presents a profile of Barry Norman, the respected film critic, journalist and TV presenter who fronted the BBC's Film programme from 1972 to 1998, and died in June.(n)

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven (1960)
programme.desc: Oppressed Mexican villagers cross the border into America looking to buy guns to defend their homes and crops from bandits. Instead they encounter an enigmatic loner who persuades them to hire a band of gunslingers to teach the villagers how to fight before the marauders return. Western, with Yul Brynner, Steve McQueen, Charles Bronson, Robert Vaughn, James Coburn and Eli Wallach.(n)

programme.title: Andy Murray: King of Queen's
programme.desc: A look at the tennis star's success in the Wimbledon warm-up event at Queen's Club in London, featuring footage of some of memorable matches from his five singles title wins. Murray has won a record five singles titles on the immaculate grass courts at Queen's. It was the scene of his first win on the ATP Tour back in 2005, and both of his Wimbledon titles were prefaced with victory at the west London venue, which is one of the most prestigious events in men's tennis and one of the highlights of the British sporting summer. The programme features some of his biggest triumphs, including his emotional victory with Feliciano Lopez in the men's doubles there last year, just a few months after major hip surgery.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monaco's only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanie's daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros' worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Typhoo Tea factory on the Wirral, which he visited back in 2017, where production has been upped to produce 109 million bags a week. In reports from the programme's archives, Cherry Healey visits a tea processing plant in Kenya, and Ruth Goodman sheds light on how Second World War troops risked their lives to brew up.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The rise and dramatic downfall of Margaret Thatcher, as told by members of her inner circle, who reveal how a political outsider won power and came to dominate the 1980s. The first episode charts how she managed to become a prominent political figure and survived the controversial policy that saw the press label her the 'milk snatcher', before challenging Edward Heath for the leadership of the Conservative Party.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a collection of memorable moments in which contestants made unusual efforts to win over the panel, from serenading them to staging a zombie attack. The brains behind a musical gift service favours red roses and flattery, while the inventor of a voice coaching massage chair hopes his creation will relax them into submission. However, the show's most successful entrant, Levi Roots, knows the best way to the Dragons' wallets is through their stomachs.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: A report on South Korea's trade in illegal spy camera footage. Pornography has been made illegal in the country, which has led criminals to begin a lucrative trade filming people without their knowledge and distributing the films to strangers or streaming it live on the internet. Stacey investigates the rising number of suicides among victims, examines how the authorities are trying to crack down on this rapidly evolving crime, and questions if the practice could take root in the UK.(n)

programme.title: When Louis Met...
programme.desc: Documentary in which Louis Theroux follows Neil and Christine Hamilton through their wrongful arrest on serious sexual assault charges - allowing him unprecedented access as the scandal and drama unfolds.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Bishop's men face a difficult choice between seeking revenge and making a profit. Felipe reveals to his sons some past cartel connections.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ and Angel both have scores to settle and sins to be punished for - but forgiving an enemy may be the hardest challenge they face.(n)

programme.title: Opera Italia
programme.desc: Conductor Antonio Pappano traces the history of Italian opera, beginning by exploring the work of Monteverdi and Rossini, as well as Handel and Mozart, who were pivotal in the development of the art form. He enlists the help of singers Juan Diego Florez, Joyce DiDonato, Danielle de Niese, Sarah Connolly and Pietro Spagnoli along the way.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 October 1989 and featuring performances by Chris Rea, Adeva and Living in a Box.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: With cinemas closed and the nation in lockdown, the UK's most-trusted screen-guides turn their attention from the big screen to the small screen for the first time. Broadcast from Mark and Simon's respective homes, the duo present a guide to the best - and worst - of streaming culture, across movies and premium TV.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Andy Crane present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 2 November 1989 and featuring performances by Deborah Harry, Phil Collins and Martika.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by groups and solo artists from the decade, beginning with music by the Kinks, Roxy Music, Elton John and Queen, plus a rendition of The Jean Genie by David Bowie recorded in January 1973.(n)

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from.(n)

programme.title: Fathers and Songs: Music for Father's Day
programme.desc: A selection of songs that celebrate fatherhood and probe the emotional complexities of a man's relationship with his son or daughter. Featured artists of past and present include Kid Creole and the Coconuts, Cat Stevens, Emmylou Harris, Paul Simon, Madonna, Peter Gabriel, Mike and the Mechanics, Suggs and the Blockheads, Neil Young, James Brown, Ozzy and Kelly Osbourne and Pigbag.(n)

programme.title: When Pop Went Epic: The Crazy World of the Concept Album
programme.desc: Rick Wakeman examines the roots of the concept album, a musical format usually based around a structured narrative, though sometimes tied together by a loose theme. He unpacks some of the most ambitious - and ridiculous - projects, from Woody Guthrie's Dustbowl Ballads to Tales from Topographic Oceans by Yes. Other works considered include the Beach Boys' Pet Sounds, George Clinton's Mothership Connection, The Wall by Pink Floyd and the Flaming Lips' Yoshimi Battles the Pink Robots.(n)

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances from the 1960s and 70s, featuring Bob Dylan, Kate Bush, Gil Scott-Heron, Kris Kristofferson, Labi Siffre, Roy Harper, Janis Ian, Gilbert O'Sullivan and Michael Chapman, as well as a duet between Graham Nash and David Crosby.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: How, between 1968 and 1972, country music responded to a US nation divided by the Vietnam War. Army captain-turned-songwriter Kris Kristofferson set a new lyrical standard, and artists like Bob Dylan and the Byrds found a recording home in Nashville. Plus, a hippie combo from California, the Nitty Gritty Band, created a landmark album that bridged the gap between the generations. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The early winter is a time of year that brings new life on the farms and Martin's sheds are full of pregnant cows - but there are two he is particularly concerned about. The vet arrives on Lewis to do an annual blood test on Sandy's Highland cattle during a Force 11 gale, while back on the mainland Sybil nearly gets carried away when she and George head to market to buy rams to breed with their ewes.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Cameras explore some of the most breathtaking places on Earth, following the lives of some of the people who call these extraordinary places home. In the first episode, cameras follow yak-herder Thokmay Lowa as he leads his herd through one of the most treacherous mountain passes in the high Himalaya. Elsewhere, the programme follows members of Inuit communities in the Canadian Arctic as they take part in the hazardous mussel harvest, and people in Siberia searching for mammoth tusks, now released from the Siberian Permafrost after thousands of years.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: The detective is irked by the arrival of a new deputy inspector and soon realises they are not cut from the same cloth. The duo investigate the kidnapping of a child and meet a beautiful Genoese architect - a rivalry for her affections ensues. Drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: Philomena (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager in 1950s Ireland becomes pregnant, and is sent to a home for 'fallen women', while her baby is forcibly taken from her and sent to America to be adopted. Fifty years later, she meets a disillusioned political journalist who attempts to reunite her with her son. Fact-based drama, starring Judi Dench and Steve Coogan.(n)

programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 October 1989 and featuring performances by Chris Rea, Adeva and Living in a Box.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Following the efforts being made by scientists, conservationists and local people in the Highlands as they work to repair the damage of the past and rebuild wild landscapes and habitats. The programme meets Dave Anderson from Forestry Commission Scotland and his colleague Simon Smith as they tag and monitor red kite and osprey chicks, gaining a unique perspective of the lives of the birds before they fly the nest. Cameras also capture the efforts of the Scottish Wildcat Action Plan team, as well as Paul Lister's project to reintroduce wolves and bears to his Alladale Wilderness Reserve.(n)

programme.title: Hamlet: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Sent by the ghost of his father to avenge his brutal death, Hamlet's mission to expose the truth is a perilous journey of madness, murder and lost love. What will ultimately become of a young man sent to kill? Paapa Essiedu plays Hamlet in Simon Godwin's critically acclaimed 2016 Royal Shakespeare Company production.(n)

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Bournemouth v Crystal Palace (Kick-off 7.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the first-ever Premier League match to be shown on terrestrial TV - and the first top-flight game broadcast live on the BBC since 1988 - at Vitality Stadium. While the visitors look comfortable in mid-table, Bournemouth resume a relegation dogfight and will be looking to improve on their performance in December's reverse fixture at Selhurst Park, when they suffered a 1-0 defeat despite Mamadou Sakho's red card for Palace in the 19th minute.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Marlborough, Wiltshire, microbiologist and amateur photographer Usha Gupta wants a garden that will be a welcoming habitat for birds, small mammals and invertebrates which will, in turn, provide great material for her photographs. Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich pay her a visit and come up with ideas to transform the featureless garden on a budget of £6,000.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger.(n)

programme.title: The Great Indoors

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Period mystery set in 19th-century New Zealand at the height of the 1860s gold rush, and based on the Man Booker Prize-winning novel by Eleanor Catton. Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the day's three Premier League fixtures, which were Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Aston Villa v Chelsea at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Sheffield United at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds were looking to record their third consecutive victory in all competitions against their bitter rivals, and the omens looked good with the Toffees' last triumph coming way back in October 2010. Villa had a similarly poor recent record against Chelsea, having lost their previous five meetings, while the Blades hadn't beaten the Magpies since November 2006.(n)

With social distancing taken into account, the two of them will spend some quality time together as best they can, look at some old family photos, awkward home video footage and tell some classic Whitehall tales.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Cathy is forced by HR to take compassionate leave and see a bereavement councillor, but she is too scared to tell Tess, so Cat says she should pretend she's going to work but come and hang out at her house instead. Cathy agrees and returns home to find Tess taking over yet more of the wedding planning, making her anxious again. Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: With a lot preying on her mind, the last thing Kayleigh needs is to find herself given the task of promoting national blackcurrant jam week. Fortunately, her car-sharing colleague John is on hand to encourage her with plenty of unwanted advice. Wry comedy, co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson.(n)

programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Bournemouth v Crystal Palace (Kick-off 7.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the first-ever Premier League match to be shown on terrestrial TV - and the first top-flight game broadcast live on the BBC since 1988 - at Vitality Stadium. While the visitors look comfortable in mid-table, Bournemouth resume a relegation dogfight and will be looking to improve on their performance in December's reverse fixture at Selhurst Park, when they suffered a 1-0 defeat despite Mamadou Sakho's red card for Palace in the 19th minute.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Period mystery set in 19th-century New Zealand at the height of the 1860s gold rush, and based on the Man Booker Prize-winning novel by Eleanor Catton. Politician Alistair Lauderback arrives at a cottage to find a woman, unconscious with opium, a Maori man - also unconscious - and a corpse. Lauderback brings the woman to town, where she is arrested for public intoxication and held at the jail. Nine months earlier, Anna Wetherell arrives in New Zealand. After a romantic first encounter, she agrees to meet with fellow passenger Emery Staines, but scheming fortune-teller Lydia Wells has other ideas. Starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie and Himesh Patel.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the day's three Premier League fixtures, which were Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Aston Villa v Chelsea at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Sheffield United at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds were looking to record their third consecutive victory in all competitions against their bitter rivals, and the omens looked good with the Toffees' last triumph coming way back in October 2010. Villa had a similarly poor recent record against Chelsea, having lost their previous five meetings, while the Blades hadn't beaten the Magpies since November 2006.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.desc: Luke doesn't listen to instructions and so loses something of great importance to Eefa. When Otta gets his hands on it, a struggles ensues to get it back.(n)

programme.title: Seonaidh (Shaun the Sheep)
programme.sub-title: Latha Sona an Tuathanaich (Happy Farmers' Day)
programme.desc: Bitzer makes plans to treat the Farmer to a restaurant-style dining experience, offering a delicious pie as the main course. However, when the pie goes missing, he makes the mistake of accepting Shaun and the flocks' offer of help in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Prosbaig
programme.desc: Housing issues on Eigg, the founding of a Scottish Motor Neurone Society by a man from Lewis diagnosed with the disease, and studies on radiation levels in island populations feature in Donald Morrison's latest collection of reports from the 1980s.(n)

programme.title: Slighe Anndra
programme.desc: John Carmichael visits London as he continues looking at the Church of Scotland's long-established congregations throughout Europe.(n)

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: A mix of hymns, psalms, readings and reflections suitable for the start of the summer months.(n)

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: Christmas approaches, and there is plenty of music, food and dancing when the staff go out to celebrate the festive season.(n)

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar visits Glasgow, where she draws a colour portrait of singer Joy Dunlop.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri is hostile towards Alasdair following his revelation in the city, but nothing can prepare her for the shock she gets when an old book, placed in an unusual location, leads her to discover another chilling secret. Life is a struggle for Tormod as he tries his hand at crofting, and Iain and his friend Anndra are left humiliated after their business pitch at the Trust.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri strives to get her life back on track as Alasdair moves in with Iseabail. Sensing Alasdair's heartache, Iseabail takes the initiative to fix their relationship by telling Màiri the truth.(n)

programme.title: Pròis Tartan (Tartan Pride)
programme.desc: The history of the first-ever international football match, which took place in 1872, and saw a Scottish side face their English rivals, setting a emotional, cultural and historical precedent that endures through to the modern day.(n)

programme.title: An Bronntanas
programme.desc: Contemporary thriller, following the lives of a local lifeboat crew, set against the backdrop of the Connemara coastline. One stormy night, the men are called out on a rescue mission to an endangered boat on which they find a single person who has been brutally murdered, as well as a cargo of more than one million euros of drugs. They decide to keep the stash for their own financial gain due to the economic depression. However, their lives spiral into a world of paranoia, violence and tragedy.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Travis has serious school news that he is scared to break to his mum, so Sharon decides to pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. However, her efforts only land the family in more trouble and it is left to Dorien to help out - using her skills of seduction. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Charlie Quirke.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Actor Warwick Davis talks to Piers about his life and career, which includes supporting roles in both the Harry Potter and Star Wars films. The actor and presenter opens up about the struggles he and his wife Sam faced when starting a family and offers an entertaining and revealing account of some of the routine realities of life as a little person, including the challenges of supermarket shopping and airline travel. He also reflects on his leading roles alongside Jennifer Aniston, Val Kilmer and Ricky Gervais, and talks about his work to support other people with dwarfism.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James visits the Shetland Islands, an area of natural beauty that he's always wanted to see. He gets to hire a vintage car to explore the area, where he learns about traditional fish salting and cooks a tasty vegetable curry at a local social enterprise project. Later on, James learns all about Shetland community spirit and cake at one of their famous Sunday teas, and finally cooks up a Shetland-inspired paella on a beautiful local beach.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team visit Canterbury to help Gurkha Hari Budha Magar, who lost the use of both his legs in Afghanistan in an IED incident. Though Hari thinks nothing of going out of his way to help others, his own garden is a challenge he has to yet to overcome, so the team transform his overlooked plot into a colourful private sanctuary, and there is a surprise appearance by Joanna Lumley.(n)

programme.title: Grow Your Own at Home with Alan Titchmarsh

programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a motorcycle courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor and Louisa make plans for their wedding, but the big day runs far from smoothly, as a drunken vicar leaves Martin with a dilemma. Meanwhile, the restaurant where the reception is to be held is flooded and Isobel goes into labour. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Since calling off his marriage to Louisa, Martin has begun to question what keeps him in Cornwall and realises that if he is ever to escape, he must conquer the blood phobia that ended his surgical career. Encouraged by aunt Joan's friend Barbara he tackles his fear, but after an accident the pair end up in hospital, where he bumps into his girlfriend from medical school. Later, the return of a familiar face shakes him up even more. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Stephanie Cole and Lia Williams.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's desire to play the guitar proves too much for his flatmates. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla insists that she has no idea who Chelsey is, but is shocked when she reminds Ms Connor that she stayed in her squat when she was sleeping rough last year. Sally refuses to sit with Tim and Geoff in the pub, and Daniel is thrilled when Adam offers to spend his birthday with him and Bertie.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Newcomer Jordan tells Carla she had better pay up for the drugs she destroyed. Adam insists Daniel should go with him to a lawyers' drinks reception in town, and Roy returns from a meeting with Shona's key worker and confirms that David's confused ex will be moving in tomorrow.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla confesses to Peter that Jordan is trying to blackmail her. Adam is pleased to see Daniel having a good time with an attractive woman, and Alya tells Imran how she suspects Sally kicked Geoff out because underneath, she knows Yasmeen is innocent. However, Faye shows her support for Geoff.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Danny is in prison, accused of murdering Peeters, but Du Plessis sets out to prove his friend's innocence - whatever the cost. Meanwhile, Ed's increasingly suspicious behaviour makes Du Plessis think he is harbouring a dark secret. With nobody around to help her, Rosie goes on a lone search for a wild cheetah, leading her into a dangerous situation.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla insists that she has no idea who Chelsey is, but is shocked when she reminds Ms Connor that she stayed in her squat when she was sleeping rough last year. Sally refuses to sit with Tim and Geoff in the pub, and Daniel is thrilled when Adam offers to spend his birthday with him and Bertie.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Newcomer Jordan tells Carla she had better pay up for the drugs she destroyed. Adam insists Daniel should go with him to a lawyers' drinks reception in town, and Roy returns from a meeting with Shona's key worker and confirms that David's confused ex will be moving in tomorrow.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla confesses to Peter that Jordan is trying to blackmail her. Adam is pleased to see Daniel having a good time with an attractive woman, and Alya tells Imran how she suspects Sally kicked Geoff out because underneath, she knows Yasmeen is innocent. However, Faye shows her support for Geoff.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: With Mandy and Vinny holed up in the salon for lockdown, things are a little less conventional. They have an endless supply of beauty products, some beers, a microwave and a karaoke machine. With Paul the main topic of conversation, Mandy and Vinny navigate through their past and soon Mandy discloses a long-held secret that has affected her whole life. Vinny at last understands her resentments and fears.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: While Nicola and Jimmy wind each other up in lockdown, she is seemingly busy with her local council business, but something is bothering her. Jimmy can't help but be aware of her sadness, but he's at a loss at what to do.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: On the school trip, Lisa struggles to get close enough to Kate to ask her what has been going on, and watches in shock as Kate continues to play the role of the tragic mother of a missing daughter. The tension amidst the parents begins to rub off on the children, and a playful accident sees Fergus rushed into hospital. Lisa follows Kate and Fergus to hospital, determined to uncover the truth. Later, Roz is forced to tell Abby that Winston has moved out. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey refuses to talk to Alf and is furious over not seeing the Queen. Curly is stunned when, out of the blue, Raquel asks him to marry her. Rita and Mavis leave for their cruise.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky tackles Steve about the money he has been withdrawing. Liz is shocked when Des tells her he slept with Raquel, while Andy walks in on Barnes and Liz drinking together.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy thinks Tina is jealous of her date with Dave, and he fritters away the money Kim gave him on a flash car. Zoe gets annoyed by Susie's sloppy living habits.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Merton is suspended following allegations of theft made by a woman he was dating, but Mike believes he is innocent and sets about clearing his name. Meanwhile, Vernon becomes concerned about Bernie's strange behaviour. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Jason Durr, Jan Francis and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.sub-title: If the Dog Collar Fits, Wear It
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.sub-title: The Morning After the Night Before
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Chrissy receives two tickets for a Frank Sinatra concert, everyone wants to be her friend. Comedy, starring Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave and Mandy's date does not go according to Mandy's plans. Jack discovers another cow has been stolen. Tina thinks Frank may have pushed Kim too far.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy accuses Des of betraying him and refuses to believe there is nothing between him and Liz. Tony swaps over to the Rovers' team. Audrey gets tipsy at a Greek restaurant.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is confused by her feelings for Des and Jim. Steve tells Vicky he feels she is acting like his probation officer. Derek is shocked when Norris asks him to be his best man.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: In the Christmas special from 2012, Robert and his family make their annual summer visit to his Scottish cousins at Duneagle Castle, leaving most of their servants at home. Edith discovers newspaper editor Michael Gregson is also on holiday in the area and may not be there just for the sketching and fishing, while personal servant O'Brien finds a kindred spirit below stairs in the form of her opposite number. Meanwhile, at Downton, Carson faces the task of keeping the staff focused on their work polishing the silverware and cleaning, but with the Granthams hundreds of miles away, minds begin to wander to other things, including the forthcoming Thirsk Country Fair.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor and Louisa make plans for their wedding, but the big day runs far from smoothly, as a drunken vicar leaves Martin with a dilemma.
