Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: On the school trip, Lisa struggles to get close enough to Kate to ask her what has been going on, and watches in shock as Kate continues to play the role of the tragic mother of a missing daughter. The tension amidst the parents begins to rub off on the children, and a playful accident sees Fergus rushed into hospital. Lisa follows Kate and Fergus to hospital, determined to uncover the truth. Later, Roz is forced to tell Abby that Winston has moved out. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Snooker v Darts
programme.desc: Short sports film.(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.sub-title: A Matter of Life and Death
programme.desc: Harry is lured to a Neolithic temple in North Africa on a mysterious mission involving six dead hippies. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A hostage is shot during an attempted armed robbery at a bank - and Quincy gets the job of defending the policeman who pulled the trigger.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Ray live it up in Australia, spending money like there's no tomorrow _ but they face tough opposition for the Daley inheritance. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents a look ahead to day four of Royal Ascot, including Commonwealth Cup. Plus, action from yesterday's Ascot Gold Cup.(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry and the Contessa agree to play along when Inspector Newman asks them to help protect a dead man. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. To make matters worse, he discovers he is being stalked by an old friend who has embarked on a vendetta. Vicki Michelle ('Allo 'Allo!) makes an early screen appearance, alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.sub-title: Sword of Honour, Blade of Death
programme.desc: A police officer is killed while listening in on a deal between the Yakuza and the Mafia. Quincy's sidekick Sam investigates, but finds himself getting deeper into trouble himself.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar visits Venice to save a damsel in distress - only to find himself plunged headlong into a seedy underworld of gangsters, intrigue and deadly revenge. Roger Moore plays the suave secret agent, with William Sylvester, Patrick Troughton and Lois Maxwell.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Ray's dreams of riches Down Under fall apart. Left penniless, Ray is forced to bar work while Arthur encounters roguish street trader Derek Collins. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Unrideables
programme.desc: A look back at motorcycling in the late 1980s and early 1990s, which is widely regarded as one of the most exciting eras in the history of two-wheel racing and featured the likes of Wayne Rainey, Eddie Lawson, Freddie Spencer, Wayne Gardner, Mick Doohan and Ron Haslam. Including action from memorable races that involved these riders competing against each other.(n)

programme.title: Steve McQueen: Motorcycle Movie Star
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Henry Cole goes on a road trip to California to uncover the often surprising life of his personal hero. Steve McQueen was Hollywood's best-paid star in his day, but motorbikes and racing meant much more to him than acting.(n)

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.sub-title: TT Best Senior Races of the Decade
programme.desc: A look back at the best stagings over the last 10 years of the meeting's blue riband event, the Senior TT.(n)

programme.desc: The comedian descends on London to entertain a celebrity crowd including Twiggy, Ringo Starr, Michael Brandon, Samantha Fox and Clive James, covering topics including ageing, parties and his idea for a new national anthem.(n)

programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.(n)

programme.desc: Arthur and Ray's dreams of riches Down Under fall apart. Left penniless, Ray is forced to bar work while Arthur encounters roguish street trader Derek Collins. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.desc: Team Bold Contender head to the formidable Pedra Branca. The location is famous for its monster waves and not many lobstermen hunt there because the swell is just too rough.(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey present the competitive angling show, joined by ex-Liverpool and Spurs footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock and former middleweight boxing champion Steve 'the Celtic Warrior' Collins. They all head back to Ali's fishing roots in Essex as the journey turns into one of humour, drama, and plenty of coarse fish, as well as a crazy sea fishing challenge to finish.(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi is joined by Tom Dove and Neil Spooner at the renowned Gigantica Lakes complex in the Champagne region of France, hoping to hook a huge fish.(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Magazine show to complement coverage of the horse racing.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits the Alaskan wilderness in search of the Iliamna Lake monster of native legend that is said to drag people down to an icy grave. Subarctic fishing is a first for the extreme angler, who secures his largest catch to date as he pursues the mysterious creature.(n)

programme.title: Football Rivalries
programme.desc: The intense rivalries between football teams across Europe, focusing on north London sides Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur.(n)

programme.title: Bend of the River (1952)
programme.desc: A trail guide saves a thief from being lynched and recruits him to help guide a party of settlers across the country. Once the homesteaders have reached their destination, the two men agree to go for supplies, but on the way back, the thief plots to sell their goods to a gold-mining camp. Western, starring James Stewart, Arthur Kennedy, Rock Hudson, Lori Nelson, Jay C Flippen and Julie Adams.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.(n)

programme.title: Bend of the River (1952)
programme.desc: A trail guide saves a thief from being lynched and recruits him to help guide a party of settlers across the country. Once the homesteaders have reached their destination, the two men agree to go for supplies, but on the way back, the thief plots to sell their goods to a gold-mining camp. Western, starring James Stewart, Arthur Kennedy, Rock Hudson, Lori Nelson, Jay C Flippen and Julie Adams.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day one of the rescheduled tournament from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the first eight frames of the opening quarter-final. The event was originally slated to take place back in March at Venue Cymru in Llandudno, but the Coronavirus outbreak meant the tournament had to be postponed, with the same venue that hosted the recent spectator-free Championship League Snooker Live being used instead. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Alex Riley and Vicki Butler-Henderson choose their favourite car from 1984, then try to convince the panel of judges that their choice is best.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hapless fool sits an exam, but when he realises he has studied the wrong maths equations, it is already too late. He also tries to stifle a sneeze in church. Rowan Atkinson stars in the first episode of the celebrated comedy, with Richard Briers, Rudolph Walker and Howard Goodall.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The buffoon wreaks havoc when he goes shopping and dines at an exclusive restaurant - and there is an unfortunate incident when he meets a member of the royal family at a film premiere. Rowan Atkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood presents action from obscure motorsports, such as bus and caravan racing and ice speedway.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day one from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the concluding eight frames of the first quarter-final. The competition sees the top eight players in the one-year ranking list competing in the third and final instalment of Coral Cup series. Neil Robertson and Judd Trump are the two players to have tasted glory in this season's series so far, having won the World Grand Prix and Players Championship respectively. However, defending champion Ronnie O'Sullivan failed to qualify this time around. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: The Nice Guys (2016)
programme.desc: A private detective investigates the murder of a porn star in 1970s Los Angeles, only to run into a hired thug employed to scare him off. However, the mysterious disappearance of a woman connected to the case forces the unlikely duo to work together, leading them to uncover a government conspiracy. Crime comedy, starring Russell Crowe and Ryan Gosling.(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: A profile of 19-time TT winner Michael Dunlop, featuring contributions from friends, team-mates and rivals.(n)

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: A look at the Padgetts racing team, who have become an integral part of the Isle of Man TT. Team leader Clive Padgett and riders Conor Cummins, Ian Hutchinson and John McGuinness reveal the secrets to their success.(n)

programme.title: The Unrideables 2: The Rainey Days
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the career of American motorcycling star Wayne Rainey, who was one of the dominant riders in the sport in the 1980s and 1990s. Featuring footage of Rainey battling against the likes of Mick Doohan, Kevin Schwantz and Eddie Lawson, along with contributions from journalists, managers and riders.(n)

programme.title: Steve McQueen: Motorcycle Movie Star
programme.desc: In the second of two programmes, Henry Cole delves further into McQueen's turbulent life and the battles he had with Hollywood. He talks bikes with US TV chat show host Jay Leno, rides McQueen's favourite Indian motorcycle and uncovers the secrets behind his famous film, The Great Escape.(n)

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)
programme.desc: The leader of a gang of bandits enslaves all the men from a Mexican village while maddened with grief over the death of his son. However, one of his hostages is a survivor from the original team of gunfighters, and his wife persuades his two remaining companions to form a new band of heroes and mount a rescue mission. Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)
programme.desc: The leader of a gang of bandits enslaves all the men from a Mexican village while maddened with grief over the death of his son. However, one of his hostages is a survivor from the original team of gunfighters, and his wife persuades his two remaining companions to form a new band of heroes and mount a rescue mission. Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day two from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the first eight frames of a quarter-final, played over the best of 17 frames. Last year, Judd Trump overcame Mark Williams in a tight encounter by a 9-8 scoreline, while Ronnie O'Sullivan made lighter work of Stuart Bingham, triumphing 9-3. The Rocket will not be involved this year, however, having failed to qualify. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: The Car Years
programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley champion cars from 1955. Vicki chooses the Mercedes 190SL, while Alex plumps for the British MGA sports car.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The hero bumbles his way through a disaster-packed day that takes him from the swimming baths and lunch in the park on to a night at the cinema with his girlfriend. Rowan Atkinson stars, with Angus Deayton and Matilda Ziegler.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson leaves his brain at home once more to play the bungling buffoon of few words. He goes shopping for a new TV, has problems with an itchy foot and takes a lucky girl for a night on the town - leaving the usual trail of chaos in his wake.(n)

programme.title: Snooker v Darts
programme.desc: Short sports film.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day two from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the second eight frames of a quarter-final, played over the best of 17 frames. The top eight players in the one-year ranking list entered this tournament hoping to win a first prize of £150,000, as well as the pride that comes with overcoming leading competitors in matches longer than in any event outside the World Championship. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: Role Models (2008)
programme.desc: Two irresponsible salesmen find themselves in trouble with the law after crashing their truck. To avoid jail, they agree to undertake community service looking after a couple of troubled kids. However, it soon seems that going to prison would have been the easier option. Comedy, starring Paul Rudd, Seann William Scott and Christopher Mintz-Plasse.(n)

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)

Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day two from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the first eight frames of a quarter-final, played over the best of 17 frames. Last year, Judd Trump overcame Mark Williams in a tight encounter by a 9-8 scoreline, while Ronnie O'Sullivan made lighter work of Stuart Bingham, triumphing 9-3. The Rocket will not be involved this year, however, having failed to qualify. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Boat enthusiast James Strangeways invites Kevin McCloud to survey progress on the construction of his new home, which his architect nephew Ben Hebblethwaite has designed to be as 'un-houselike' as possible. James has spent the past 35 years travelling the canals and waterways of Britain, and despite never liking houses, has decided now is the time to put down roots on dry land. Ben hoped a home standing on stilts above a marsh in East Sussex, and incorporating ship-like qualities such as a keel-shaped roof, will be enough to satisfy his boat-mad uncle. However, Ben's contractor goes into liquidation a few months into the project, taking £87,000 of the £300,000 budget, and when James decides to make a few tweaks to the design, alarm-bells begin to ring for the young architect.(n)

The duo love cooking and entertaining and are searching for a holiday home where they can invite all their friends. programme.desc: Craig Charles assesses Samsung's much-anticipated folding Galaxy Z Flip, while Jon Bentley gives Gogglebox star Sandra Martin three affordable 65-inch televisions to check out. Ortis Deley spends the day with his three-year-old daughter at London's annual Toy Fair where they explore the hottest techy playthings on offer for 2020. The team also revisits 1971, a year when floppy disks were not only a thing but were actually floppy and Craig continues his attempt to turn a house into a smart home. Plus, Harry Wallop has his latest tips on how to save money using tech.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a train journey across Sri Lanka along one of the world's best-preserved Victorian railways. The trip begins in the coastal city of Colombo and moves on to the country's cultural and spiritual heart in Kandy. The journey continues through a a very English town that produces some of the world's finest teas, coming to a stop at a hill station surrounded by tropical forests close to one of the world's most spectacular viaducts - the Nine Arch Bridge.(n)

programme.title: A Greek Odyssey with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: Bettany visits the sacred isle of Delos, where it has been forbidden for anyone to permanently reside since ancient times. Wandering the ruins, Bettany discovers a dark history to this incredible site as she explores how the Romans turned Delos into a marketplace for their slave trade. She then moves on to Ikaria, an island famous for its long-lived population, and gets a lesson in wine-making. As she moves on to Mykonos, Bettany's boat is caught in a storm reaching a 10 on the Beaufort scale.(n)

programme.title: The Thames: Britain's Great River with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actor and presenter embarks on a 200-mile journey from source to sea to discover what makes the Thames one of the greatest rivers in the world. He begins by tracking down the source of the waterway, as it emerges from the ground as a pure spring in a Gloucestershire field. He then follows the river as it winds its way through the Thames valley, stopping off at Henley-on-Thames, and then on towards the palace at Hampton Court.(n)

programme.title: Great Paintings of the World with Andrew Marr
programme.sub-title: The Fighting Temeraire by JMW Turner
programme.desc: Andrew Marr visits the National Gallery to uncover the story behind JMW Turner's The Fighting Temeraire. The painting, depicting a ship that played a vital role in the Battle of Trafalgar, proved a pivotal moment in the artist's career and remains a national treasure to this day, adorning the £20 note and topping a recent poll to choose the nation's favourite British painting.(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Traversing the Baltic on board the Viking Sky, Jane takes full advantage of the ship's amenities. Her first stop is Tallinn in Estonia, a medieval town with much ancient architecture. Next up is St Petersburg in Russia, where Jane heads to the Hermitage Museum, hoping to spot the cats that traditionally live in the basement. The third stop is Helsinki in Finland, where Jane meets a woman whose ambition is to kiss a brown bear. Finally, the ship pulls into the Swedish capital of Stockholm for one of the highlights of the singer's journey - a guided tour around the Abba Museum.(n)

programme.title: King George VI: The Accidental King
programme.desc: Documentary examining how a shy boy went on to become a national inspiration in Britain's darkest days during the Second World War. George VI was determined to return the monarchy to a state of steadfast stability after the brief reign of his brother Edward, and his lasting legacy is seen to this day in the role his daughter Elizabeth plays as a devoted monarch to her subjects.(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow charts the life of Greek artist Domenicos Theotocopolous, dubbed El Greco, one of the most distinctive painters of the 16th century. His work was considered to be ahead of its time - so much so that it was all but ignored for 300 years. El Greco developed a highly individualistic style that eventually proved enormously influential, paving the way for the Baroque and providing the inspiration for 20th-century expressionism.(n)
